divergent_child.mechanism: Rather than allocating on same-day stress breadth, measure how quickly range/volume shocks mean-revert over several days and allocate only when shock half-life shortens across defensive and risk ETFs.
divergent_child.title: ETF stress recovery half-life allocator from volume-normalized range decay

bad_result_policy.note: If rejected, no refinement/extension/direct inversion is suggested.

costs_slippage.handling: qfa backtest has no transaction-cost parameter; post-cost proxy subtracts 5 bps times estimated one-way daily target-weight turnover.

data_source: Alpaca real OHLCV via qfa/AlpacaGateway; --data-csv not used

db_used.retention_note: Temporary SQLite DBs deleted after qfa JSON capture.

decision_rationale: Post-cost random-window median Sharpe 0.61968753 is positive and full-period post-cost Sharpe 0.45490526 is positive; however worst random Sharpe -0.25617409 and full-period max drawdown -0.17241563 argue for watchlist rather than acceptance.

orthogonality.method: Pearson correlation of daily qfa equity returns from retained latest.json curves where available

random_period_protocol.selection: deterministic pseudo-random/staggered windows spanning 2021-2025, 18 months plus one longer stress window

warnings: qfa applies no native costs/slippage; costed metrics are a post-processing proxy.
Alpaca daily feed returned bars through the requested end where available; no local CSV was supplied.
The model remains long-only and may retain equity beta during abrupt shocks.
